modules.title: The Milestone Philosophy
modules.description: Understand the foundation of the application and why it uses a hierarchical structure.

steps.description: Milestone replaces flat to-do lists with a strict hierarchy: **Workbooks** (Your main goal) -> **Categories** (Sections/Phases) -> **Modules & Steps** (The actionable tasks). This structure reduces cognitive load and makes massive goals achievable.
Your data is not stored on a cloud server. Everything lives securely in your browser's `localStorage`. This means the app is lightning-fast, highly private, and works 100% offline.

modules.title: Mastering the Interface
modules.description: Learn to navigate the UI, reduce distractions, and customize your viewing experience.

steps.description: Click the Moon/Sun icon in the top right corner. Use Light Mode for daytime focus and Dark Mode for late-night coding or reading sessions to reduce eye strain.

steps.text: Collapse the Sidebar for Deep Work
steps.description: When reading long markdown notes, the sidebar can be distracting. Click the 'hamburger' menu icon (three lines) next to the dashboard back button to collapse the sidebar and enter full-screen reading mode.
At the bottom of every module, you will find 'Next' and 'Previous' buttons. Use these to seamlessly flow through your curriculum without needing to open the sidebar.
Click the 'Dashboard' back button at the top of the sidebar, or click the Milestone logo in the top left corner, to return to your main Workbook grid.

modules.title: Creating Your First Environment
modules.description: Build a personalized workbook tailored to your specific project or learning goal.

steps.description: On the main dashboard, click the blue `New Workbook` button. Give your project a clear, actionable title like 'Mastering React.js' or 'Marathon Training Prep'.

Pick a Theme Color. This color will instantly apply to all progress bars, buttons, and active states inside the workbook. Next, assign a FontAwesome icon (e.g., `fa-brands fa-js`) to represent it visually.

This is crucial: Change the default words to match your goal. 

- Learning to code? Use **Labs** and **Checkpoints**.
- Reading a book? Use **Chapters** and **Pages**.
- Exercising? Use **Weeks** and **Workouts**.

The entire app will rewrite its UI to speak your chosen language.

modules.title: Managing Existing Workbooks
modules.description: Learn how to edit or safely delete your high-level projects.

steps.description: Hover over any Workbook card on the dashboard. Click the small Pencil icon in the top right corner to change the title, color, icon, or terminology at any time.
Hover over a Workbook card and click the Trash icon. **Warning:** This will permanently delete the workbook and every single module, step, and piece of progress inside it.

modules.title: Categories and Modules
modules.description: Organize your knowledge logically to create a clear roadmap.

steps.description: Inside a Workbook, click `Add New Module` in the sidebar. In the 'Category/Section' field, type a new name like 'Beginner Concepts'. Categories group your modules together visually.
On the main category overview screen, hover over a category title. Click the Pencil icon to rename it, or the Trash icon to delete it (which also deletes all modules inside it!).
Modules are the specific lessons. Give your module an action-oriented title (e.g., 'Understanding Variables') and assign it a unique icon.

modules.title: Steps and Rich Markdown
modules.description: Write beautiful, detailed checklists using markdown formatting.

steps.description: At the bottom of the module form, add your specific tasks. Break big tasks down! If a task takes more than 2 hours, make it a separate step.
Use the Up and Down arrow buttons next to the step inputs in the module form to perfectly sequence your learning path.
The description box is a powerful notepad. Use `**bold**`, `*italics*`, `> blockquotes`, and `- lists` to format your notes perfectly.
Writing a coding curriculum? Use backticks to embed code. Inline code looks like `npm start`. Triple backticks allow you to paste massive multi-line configuration files safely.
To keep the UI clean, step descriptions are hidden by default. Click the Chevron (arrow) icon next to a step title to expand and read your rich markdown notes.

modules.title: The Three Levels of Progress
modules.description: Understand how Milestone motivates you mathematically.

steps.description: When you check a box, look at the sidebar. The percentage next to your current module will instantly increase. Hit 100% and it turns into a green checkmark.
Click the 'Back to Categories' button. You will see progress bars for every section. This macro view shows you exactly where you are falling behind.
Look at the top of your screen. The master progress bar calculates the total percentage of every step in the entire workbook. This is your ultimate finish line.

modules.title: Managing Your Completion State
modules.description: How to handle iterative learning and reviewing.

steps.description: Forgot a concept? Simply uncheck the step. Your progress bars will dynamically recalculate downwards, allowing you to honestly track what you actually know.
Finished a course but want to take it again to solidify your knowledge? Click the `Reset WB` button in the sidebar footer. This instantly unchecks all steps so you can start fresh.

modules.title: Backups and Factory Resets
modules.description: Because your data is local, you must manage your own backups.

steps.description: Click the `Settings` gear icon in the top right of the main dashboard. Click `Export All Data`. This downloads a file to your computer containing everything. **Do this regularly!**
Changed browsers? Cleared your cache accidentally? Go to `Settings`, click `Import Data (Restore)`, select your backup file, and your entire workspace will reappear.
Located in the settings menu, this button will permanently delete ALL data from your browser's local storage. Use this only if you want to completely destroy your workspace.

modules.title: Sharing and Collaboration
modules.description: Share your hard work with friends without risking your data.

steps.description: On the main dashboard, hover over any workbook card and look for the `Share` (link) icon. Click it to generate a unique URL.
When someone opens your shared link, Milestone enters Read-Only mode. They can read your markdown and browse your modules, but they cannot check boxes, edit text, or delete your data.
Alternatively, you can export a workbook as JSON and email it to a friend. They can use the `Import` button inside an empty workbook to load your curriculum onto their machine.

modules.title: Generating Courses with AI
modules.description: Use ChatGPT or Gemini to build 50-step courses in 10 seconds.

steps.description: Open any workbook, click `Import` in the sidebar footer, and click 'View Sample JSON Format'. Copy that block of code.
Go to ChatGPT and type: 'I want to learn [Topic]. Create a 20-step learning roadmap for me. Output the result strictly as a raw JSON array matching this exact schema: [Paste Schema Here]'
Copy the AI's JSON output, go back to the Milestone Import modal, paste it into the text area, and hit `Import JSON`. The app will instantly build the entire course.

modules.title: Hacking Milestone for Unique Uses
modules.description: Milestone is a chameleon. Change the terminology to use it for anything.

steps.description: Set the step title as the Question. Hide the Answer in the markdown description. Expand to reveal, and check the box if you get it right.
Set Module Term to `Quest` and Item Term to `Objective`. Give the workbook a sword icon. Completing chores now fills up your 'Experience' bar.
Create categories for 'Weeks'. Create modules for 'Days'. Your steps are your daily habits (Drink Water, Meditate). Track your discipline percentage over the month.
Make the Module title the Question. Make the Steps the multiple-choice options. Use the markdown descriptions to explain why an option is right or wrong.
